strategy_recommendation.default_path: Continue the in-repo staged WPHX PHP Adapter IR / reusable PHP core IR / WordPress-profile lane for new public WordPress PHP emission.
strategy_recommendation.stock_haxe_php_role: Remain private implementation emitter and std/php runtime behavior oracle until a later backend-promotion ADR moves that responsibility.
strategy_recommendation.extracted_reflaxe_php: Do not start a parallel extracted target now; begin extraction hygiene and wait for backend-scale pressure plus a later ADR.
strategy_recommendation.backend_promotion: Open a backend-promotion ADR only with broad expression lowering, repeated generic core features, runtime/std ownership, mixed-template/source-map ownership, or stock-private-output failure evidence.

direct_answers.answer: Yes. The staged WPHX PHP path remains the best route for the next phase; it matches the PRD's public PHP topology constraints and now has usable-compiler evidence.
Mostly separated cleanly, but profile growth is now significant enough to require a recurring core/profile promotion audit.
Conceptually strong, but add trend-based gates for profile adapter growth, helper/bootstrap reliance, copy/patch surfaces, and snapshot coverage.
Do not start an extracted reflaxe.php target now. Keep WPHX PHP in-repo and begin extraction hygiene until backend-scale evidence accumulates.
High enough for current bounded claims, not yet high enough for broad public distribution without plugin/theme reflection, packaged stack-trace, source-map, warning, and readability gates.

Healthy: core-lowering, whole-file class-http pilot, snapshots, runtime/std strategy, zero raw blocks, oEmbed static-property IR. Drift warnings: large wp-embed/wp-http profile adapter accumulation, high helper/bootstrap counts, remaining runner copy/patch surfaces, script switches in compiler core.
Before replacing stock Haxe PHP private output, require a private-emitter pilot, stock-vs-WPHX differential runtime/std suite, boot/debug/source-map evidence, and an ADR moving ownership.

Full class-wp-http/embed/oembed ownership requires complete file inventories, generated whole-file original paths, all-symbol ABI/reflection, broad oracle/candidate behavior, packaged installed gates, selected PHPUnit pass/pass, unsupported=[], and no copied shell/runner patch fallback.

Promote native array reads/writes/isset/empty, by-reference mutation, static/dynamic members, callables/closures, file-scope include/segment IR, exceptions, attributes/signatures, and source/debug mapping into reusable core IR.

The pivot is falsified if public slices repeatedly need raw PHP/copies/source contortions, profile adapters grow faster than core IR, generated PHP fails plugin/theme/debug gates, bootstrap cannot be made WordPress-compatible, or whole-file installed gates fail despite isolated probes.

architectural_risks_and_missing_evidence: Profile adapter accumulation: 54 WordPress-profile method adapters is acceptable only with active core-IR promotion pressure.
Stock/Haxe helper and bootstrap reliance remains high: 59 helper metadata sites and 97 bootstrap metadata sites.
Runner copy/patch surfaces remain at 6 and should trend down before broad claims.
Generated PHP quality is not yet proven against plugin/theme reflection, subclassing, operator debugging, packaged stack traces, or broad source-map expectations.
Mixed template/caller-scope evidence is still minimized segment-model evidence, not existing Core template ownership.
Adoption CI should become continuous and explicitly list post-pivot evidence manifests included/excluded from the gate.
Prompt evidence list has a whole-file receipt path mismatch: actual receipt is wphx-comp-php-whole-file-pilot.v1.json.

ADR-015: Add a profile-accretion gate requiring every new WordPress-profile adapter to classify as profile-only, core-IR candidate, temporary bridge, or backend-promotion pressure.
Add quantitative/trend triggers for backend-promotion review when profile adapters grow without core IR promotion or when generic constructs stay in profile code.
Add extraction-readiness checklist but explicitly state it is not extraction approval.
ADR-016: Turn WPHX-COMP-PHP-ADOPTION-CI into a continuous adoption gate for every new public WordPress slice.
Require each new public slice to produce core IR promotion, profile-only ABI justification, stock fallback reduction, bootstrap/helper reduction, or filed backend-pressure evidence.
Require adoption CI to list any WPHX PHP evidence manifests it does not execute directly.
ADR-017: Add a private-emitter replacement ladder before stock Haxe PHP can be abandoned.
Distinguish direct reuse, source-adapted reuse with hashes, and deliberate replacement for every borrowed runtime/std component.
Require source-map/debug and runtime/std differential probes before moving private implementation output to WPHX PHP.

stop_or_pivot_criteria: Inline raw PHP or long-lived adapter templates return for public bodies.
Runner copy/patch surfaces increase instead of decrease.
Generated public PHP fails plugin/theme reflection or packaged stack-trace fixtures.
WordPress-profile adapter count grows materially while reusable core IR feature coverage is flat.
Two independent Core domains need broad arbitrary-Haxe expression lowering or runtime/std ownership.
Stock Haxe PHP private output becomes a quality bottleneck and cannot be fixed or bounded.
Whole-file pilots require file-scope Haxe expression lowering, mixed templates, source maps, or dynamic dispatch beyond Adapter IR.
Adoption CI stops tracking the actual evidence surface used by new slices.

artifacts.role: human-readable oracle response created by this receipt
oracle index summary updated with accepted pivot-soundness response

claims: The oracle response recommends continuing the WPHX PHP staged custom compiler path after ADR-015, ADR-016, ADR-017, the usable-compiler gate, and first post-pivot public WordPress slices.
The response recommends keeping WPHX PHP in-repo and not beginning a parallel extracted reflaxe.php target yet.
The response identifies profile adapter growth, helper/bootstrap reliance, runner copy/patch surfaces, and missing plugin/theme/debug gates as the main risks.
The response recommends specific amendments to ADR-015, ADR-016, and ADR-017 before full backend promotion, stock-target abandonment, or mature reflaxe.php scope claims.
non_claims: This receipt does not claim WPHX PHP is a mature arbitrary-Haxe PHP backend.
This receipt does not claim stock Haxe PHP can be abandoned as private implementation emitter.
This receipt does not claim full class-wp-http.php, class-wp-embed.php, class-wp-oembed.php, installed WordPress distribution, live transport, or Gutenberg/browser ownership.
This receipt does not update the Beads database by itself; Beads reflection must be performed through bd in the real repository.
